title: 30o: compute_sha256_hex uppercase then to_lowercase
description: Location: crates/jmap-chat/src/blob.rs lines 189-198. compute_sha256_hex builds hex output via hex_digit() which produces uppercase A-F (lines 63-64), then calls .to_lowercase() on the final string. This is not dead code but is internally contradictory: the digit-building function produces uppercase, the caller immediately lowercases. Why burden: a maintainer reading hex_digit() sees uppercase output and must separately find the to_lowercase() call to understand the actual output contract. If hex_digit() is ever changed to produce lowercase directly, the to_lowercase() becomes a silent no-op that may be missed in review. Suggested fix: either produce lowercase in hex_digit() directly (change b'A' to b'a') and remove the to_lowercase() call, making the intent clear at the source.
close_reason: fixed: hex_digit now takes uppercase bool, compute_sha256_hex passes false
